services.title: Context Engineering Services
services.description: Intelligence before code. We engineer the context layer — system constraints, domain knowledge, and task boundaries — so your agents think right before they act.

services.title: Agent Engineering & Development
services.description: Your core AI capability, engineered right. From single-agent pipelines to multi-agent orchestration — we design, build, and ship production-grade agents that actually work in the real world.

services.title: Agent Security
services.description: Most companies treat agent security as an afterthought. We make it a dedicated lifecycle stage — because one prompt injection can undo months of engineering.

services.title: Agentic AI Assurance Services
services.description: Ship with confidence, not crossed fingers. We validate your agents for reliability, fairness, and trust — because an unverified agent is a liability, not an asset.

blueprints.title: Appointment Intelligence
blueprints.description: An intelligent multi-agent system that transforms hospital appointment scheduling from a fragmented, manual process into a seamless, context-aware experience — without touching clinical decisions.

pipeline.description: Patient initiates via chat, voice, or web portal

pipeline.role: Classifies intent, extracts key info, routes to appropriate worker
Gathers insurance details, preferences, accessibility needs, symptoms in plain language
Checks doctor availability, matches specialty, suggests optimal slots

pillars.context: Patient history context, department knowledge base, insurance provider rules
pillars.build: Supervisor + 3 worker agents, MCP tool integrations (EHR, Calendar, SMS)
pillars.secure: PHI/PII redaction, insurance data boundaries, HIPAA-compliant guardrails, input sanitization
pillars.assure: Scheduling accuracy validation, agent denial rate for edge cases, bias detection (equitable wait times)

demoConversation.message:
Welcome to Metro General Hospital's scheduling assistant. I'm the Supervisor Agent — I'll understand what you need and route you to the right specialist agent.

What can I help you with today?

choices.label: I need to see a cardiologist
I'd like a general health checkup
I need to reschedule an existing appointment

demoConversation.message:
I'm the Intake Agent. Before I hand you off to scheduling, I need a few details to find the best match.

Which insurance provider are you with?
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Supervisor] Intent: new-appointment | Specialty: cardiology | Priority: routine
[Supervisor] Routing → Intake Agent (need insurance + patient details before scheduling)

choices.label: BlueCross BlueShield
Aetna
I'm self-pay / no insurance

demoConversation.message:
Got it — BlueCross PPO is in-network here. A couple more things:

Do you have any scheduling preferences or accessibility needs?
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Intake] Insurance: BlueCross PPO — verified in-network with Metro General
[Intake] Building patient profile... need: preferences, accessibility
[Security] PII boundary check — insurance ID will not be stored in conversation log

choices.label: Morning appointments preferred, no special needs
Afternoons only — I need wheelchair accessible rooms
No preference, just the earliest available

demoConversation.message:
I'm the Scheduling Agent. I've checked availability across our cardiology department. Here are your best options:

**Dr. Sarah Chen** — Mon, Mar 10 at 9:30 AM (next available)
**Dr. James Park** — Wed, Mar 12 at 10:15 AM
**Dr. Priya Sharma** — Thu, Mar 13 at 8:45 AM

All accept BlueCross PPO. Which works best?
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Intake] Patient profile complete → {specialty: cardiology, insurance: BlueCross PPO, preference: mornings, accessibility: none}
[Intake] Output: Structured Patient Profile — handing off to Scheduling Agent
[Scheduling] Received profile. Querying tools...
[Tool Call] EHR System → checking cardiologists accepting BlueCross PPO
[Tool Call] Calendar API → fetching available morning slots, next 2 weeks
[Tool Call] Department Directory → Cardiology dept, floor 3

choices.label: Dr. Sarah Chen — Monday 9:30 AM
Dr. Priya Sharma — Thursday 8:45 AM
Can you check next week instead?

demoConversation.message:
All set! Here's your appointment summary:

**Dr. Sarah Chen — Cardiology**
Monday, March 10 at 9:30 AM
Metro General Hospital, Floor 3, Room 312

I've sent a confirmation to your email and scheduled an SMS reminder for the day before. You can also view this in your patient portal.

**Prep instructions**: No caffeine 12 hours before visit. Bring recent blood work if available.
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Scheduling] Appointment confirmed: Dr. Sarah Chen, Mon Mar 10, 9:30 AM, Cardiology Floor 3, Room 312
[Scheduling] Output: Appointment Confirmed — routing to Notification Agent
[Notification] Preparing confirmation across channels...
[Tool Call] Email Service → sending confirmation to patient@email.com
[Tool Call] SMS Gateway → sending reminder scheduled for Sun Mar 9, 6:00 PM
[Tool Call] Patient Portal → appointment visible in patient dashboard

choices.label: Can I also get a reminder 2 hours before?
What should I bring to the appointment?
That's everything, thank you!

demoConversation.message:
Appointment scheduled successfully! Here's what happened behind the scenes:

**4 agents collaborated** in under 30 seconds — Supervisor classified your intent, Intake built your profile, Scheduling queried 3 external systems, and Notification dispatched across 3 channels.
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Pipeline Complete]
Context: Patient history context loaded, insurance rules applied, department knowledge queried
Build: Supervisor + 3 worker agents orchestrated via MCP protocol
Secure: PII boundaries enforced, insurance data not persisted, input sanitized
Assure: Scheduling accuracy validated, wait time equity checked, no bias detected

blueprints.title: First-Gen College Navigator
blueprints.description: A multilingual AI navigator that bridges the information gap for first-generation college students — guiding them through admissions, scholarships, and documents that privileged students learn from family.

pipeline.description: Student asks in their preferred language via chat

pipeline.role: Understands intent, assesses student profile, routes to specialist
Matches student background to programs, scholarships, financial aid options
Explains required documents, tracks submission status, sends deadline reminders
Answers questions in simple language, provides step-by-step guidance

pillars.context: Student background (first-gen status, economic situation, academic interests), institutional knowledge, regional language context
pillars.build: Navigator supervisor + 3 specialized agents, multilingual NLP, RAG over institutional knowledge
pillars.secure: Minor's data protection, economic data sensitivity, no demographic profiling for steering
pillars.assure: Bias detection (agent must not steer based on demographics), recommendation fairness, hallucination prevention on eligibility info

demoConversation.message:
Hi! I'm the Navigator — an AI assistant built to help first-generation college students find their path. I speak multiple languages and I'm here to make this process less overwhelming.

What's on your mind today?
demoConversation.reasoning: [Navigator] Initializing — language: English (auto-detected) — awaiting student intent

choices.label: I want to find scholarships I qualify for
I don't know what documents I need for applications
I got accepted but I'm confused about financial aid

demoConversation.message:
I'm the Eligibility Agent. I'll match you with scholarships and aid you actually qualify for — no generic lists.

To find the best matches, are you currently in high school or already enrolled in college?
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Navigator] Intent: scholarship-search | Profile: first-gen, needs financial support
[Navigator] Routing → Eligibility Agent (scholarship + financial aid matching)
[Security] Minor's data protection check — no demographic profiling for steering

choices.label: High school senior — applying this year
High school junior — planning ahead
Already in college — looking for ongoing aid

demoConversation.message:
Great — here's what I found for high school seniors who are first-generation:

**Scholarships** (3 strong matches):
- First in Family Scholarship — $5,000/year (deadline: Apr 15)
- Gates Millennium Scholars — full tuition (deadline: Jan 15)
- Coca-Cola First Gen Fund — $2,500 one-time (deadline: Mar 30)

**Financial Aid**:
- You likely qualify for Federal Pell Grant (up to $7,395/year)
- Most state universities have first-gen fee waivers

Want me to dig into any of these, or check what documents you'd need?
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Eligibility] Profile: HS senior, first-gen, seeking scholarships
[Tool Call] Scholarship Database → filtering: first-generation, HS senior, need-based
[Tool Call] Financial Aid Rules → checking FAFSA eligibility indicators
[Tool Call] University Catalog → programs with first-gen support services
[Assure] Bias check — recommendations not influenced by ZIP code or ethnicity

choices.label: Tell me more about the Gates Scholarship
What documents do I need for these?
Can you check scholarships for a specific major?

demoConversation.message:
I'm the Document Agent. Here's your personalized checklist:

**Must-have documents**:
- FAFSA (Free Application) — opens Oct 1, priority deadline Mar 1
- High school transcript (request from counselor)
- Parent/guardian tax return (or non-filer statement)
- Personal essay (most scholarships require one)

**For Gates Scholarship specifically**:
- Community service record
- Two recommendation letters

**Your Application Readiness Score: 2 of 6 items ready**

Want me to explain any of these in simpler terms?
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Eligibility] Output: Personalized Options List — 3 scholarships + federal aid matched
[Eligibility] Student chose document guidance → routing to Document Agent
[Document] Loading checklist for: first-gen HS senior, scholarship + FAFSA applications
[Tool Call] Document Checklist API → generating personalized requirements
[Tool Call] Calendar → mapping deadlines

choices.label: What's a non-filer statement? My parents don't file taxes
How do I request my transcript?
Set deadline reminders for me

demoConversation.message:
Great question — this confuses a lot of people, so you're not alone.

A **non-filer statement** is simply a letter saying "my parents didn't earn enough to file taxes." Here's exactly what to do:

1. **Write a short letter** — "I, [Parent Name], did not file a federal tax return for [year] because my income was below the filing threshold."
2. **Parent signs it**
3. **Optional but helpful**: Get it notarized at your local library (usually free)

The FAFSA also has a checkbox for this — select "Will not file" under parent tax status.

Many first-gen families are in this situation. It will *not* hurt your application — it actually strengthens your case for need-based aid.
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Document] Student needs guidance on non-filer statement → routing to Counselor Agent
[Counselor] Loading: FAQ Knowledge Base → "non-filer statement" explanation
[Counselor] Adjusting language: plain English, no jargon, step-by-step
[Assure] Hallucination check — verifying against official FAFSA guidelines

choices.label: That's really helpful. What's next on my checklist?
Can you explain FAFSA in simple terms too?
I think I'm ready — summarize everything for me

demoConversation.message:
Here's your complete summary:

**3 scholarships** matched to your profile (total potential: $14,895+/year)
**Application Readiness**: 2 of 6 documents ready
**Next deadline**: Coca-Cola First Gen Fund — Mar 30

4 agents worked together to get you here — Navigator understood your needs, Eligibility searched 3 databases, Document built your checklist, and Counselor explained everything in plain language.
demoConversation.reasoning:
[Pipeline Complete]
Context: First-gen student profile, institutional knowledge base, financial aid rules applied
Build: Navigator + 3 specialized agents, RAG over scholarship database
Secure: Minor's data protected, no demographic steering, economic data kept private
Assure: Bias detection passed — recommendations based on eligibility only, not demographics
